page: Amazon Web Services c6a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. This x86_64 architecture system features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor configuration includes dedicated CPU allocation, 32 KB of L1 data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight the multi-core processing capabilities of this hardware profile. The system achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Single-core performance is strong, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core and PassMark CPU physics benchmarks. Memory performance is mixed, with small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 25 percent, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via hardware flags such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, alongside vector capabilities like AVX.

In application-specific workloads, the c6a.24xlarge demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations. It also achieves top-tier performance in prompt processing for small language models with 135M parameters and strong performance in text generation for the same model class, though performance scales down to average levels for larger 7B and 70B parameter models. Given its compute-heavy resource ratio and lack of local storage, this instance represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for high-density compute workloads, batch processing, distributed analytics, and web serving where external storage is utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores running at 3.6 GHz, the system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. The instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads, distributed analytics, and batch processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six virtual cores and high-speed memory for intensive multi-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.6 GHz.
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 2.0 GB per core resource density.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cryptographic encryption capabilities.
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for software compilation, batch processing, and distributed compute workloads.
